content.title: The days of Dhul-Hijjah are running out fast!
content.subtitle: Don't miss out on the year's greatest opportunity!
content.body:
In Surah Al-Fajr, Allah Subhanahu Wa Ta'ala swears by the ten nights, which refers to the first ten days of Dhul Hijjah. This alone reflects the immense significance of the season we are currently passing through. Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ) said:

"There are no days in which righteous deeds are more beloved to Allah than these ten days." — Sunan Abi Dawud 2438, Sahih al-Bukhari 969

While preparation for Qurbani and Eid is essential, let it not become our only focus. Let’s intentionally utilize the remaining days with these beautiful deeds:

  • Elevate Your Salat: Beyond the 5 daily obligatory Salat, let's intentionally add Nafl prayers. Perform Salat ud-Duha after sunrise, and arrive at the masjid 5 minutes early to offer Dukhulul Masjid.

  • Fasting & Tahajjud: Fasting the first 9 days of Dhul Hijjah is highly recommended. Above all, ensure you fast on the Day of Arafah (9th Dhul Hijjah), which expiates the minor sins of both the previous and upcoming year. Wake up slightly early for Sahari to catch the blessings of Tahajjud.

  • Quran Recitation: Use this spiritual season to increase your daily quota of Quran recitation.

  • Sadaqah (Charity): With Eid approaching, extend your hands to the needy around you. Try to give a small amount in Sadaqah every single day of this blessed decade.

May Allah grant us all the ability to make the most of this peak season of Ibadah. Ameen.

footer_button.hint: You can send a gift (Hadiya) to support Muslims Day's Research & Development for the month of Dhul-Hijjah. Don't miss out on the reward of helping countless people practice their Deen!
